description: Joint evaluation of pge-research → pge-plan handoff and value-add

evals.research_input: User wants to add a dark mode toggle. Repo has CSS custom properties already.
evals.research_expected: Brief with READY_FOR_PLAN, one option (extend existing custom properties), 0 questions, affected areas: 3 CSS files + 1 toggle component.
evals.plan_expected: Consumes brief as-is. Engineering Review is lightweight (scope check: 4 files, no complexity gate). Adopts research recommendation without re-brainstorming. Produces 2-3 issues. No questions asked.
joint_check: Plan Intent matches research Intent verbatim or near-verbatim
Plan Target Areas matches research Affected Areas
Plan does NOT re-explore files research already cited with file:line
Engineering Review adds value beyond research (scope challenge, architecture check) without duplicating findings

evals.scenario: Complex task, research provides options, plan must review
evals.research_input: User wants to migrate state management. Research found 3 options (Redux Toolkit, Zustand, Jotai) with tradeoffs. Recommended Zustand. 12 files affected. 1 non-blocking open question about test mocking strategy.
evals.research_expected: Brief with READY_FOR_PLAN, 3 options with evidence, recommendation: Zustand, open question (blocks_plan: no).
evals.plan_expected: Consumes brief. Adopts Zustand as strong default. Engineering Review is substantive: scope challenge (12 files → can we migrate incrementally?), architecture (state boundaries, data flow during partial migration), existing solutions (any existing state patterns to preserve?). Complexity gate triggers. Plan may propose phased migration. Open question carried to Risks.
joint_check: Plan adopts Zustand without re-comparing all 3 options
Plan Engineering Review challenges the APPROACH (incremental vs big-bang), not the CHOICE (Zustand vs others)
Research open question appears in Plan Risks / Open Questions
Plan adds engineering depth that research did not cover (failure modes, migration strategy, phasing)

evals.scenario: Research signals blocker, plan must stop
evals.research_input: User wants to add real-time notifications. Research found the intent is ambiguous (push notifications? in-app? email?) and asked a goal-sharpening question. User did not answer.
evals.research_expected: Brief with NEEDS_INFO, 1 blocking open question (blocks_plan: yes).
evals.plan_expected: Gate check detects research_route: NEEDS_INFO. Stops immediately. Does NOT produce a plan. Tells user to resolve the research blocker first.
joint_check: Plan does NOT attempt to plan around the ambiguity
Plan does NOT ask its own version of the same question
Plan clearly references the research brief and its incomplete state

evals.scenario: Research recommendation contradicted by plan exploration
evals.research_input: Research recommended approach A (add new service). Plan exploration discovers that the codebase already has a similar service that could be extended instead.
evals.research_expected: Brief with READY_FOR_PLAN, recommendation: new service.
evals.plan_expected: Consumes brief. During Phase 2 Explore, discovers existing service. Engineering Review Scope Challenge Q1 ('What existing code already solves this?') flags the existing service. Existing Solutions Check confirms prior art. Plan OVERRIDES research recommendation with rationale. Records why research recommendation was rejected.
joint_check: Plan does NOT blindly follow research recommendation when evidence contradicts it
Plan records the override with clear rationale in Approach Decision
Plan credits research for the original recommendation but explains what changed
Engineering Review is the mechanism that caught the issue (not ad-hoc exploration)
